sections.title: Danger - Danger To Life From Electric Shock Indicates Life-Threatening Situations Due To Electric Current. Failure To Follow The Safety Instructions May Result In Serious Injury Or Death. The Work Being Performed May Only Be Carried Out By A Qualified Electrician Who Has Been Trained On The System.
Precautionary Measures During Use Danger Of Electric Shock.
Observe The Supply Voltage Use The Device Only With The Specified Voltage.
Warning - Danger Of Electric Shock And Fire
You Must Not Reach Into The Rtvision.T System - Danger Of Electric Shock And Fire
Danger - Electric Shock During Maintenance Must Be Observed Before And During System Maintenance
Note - Material Damage Operation Of The System

Caution - Material Damage Electrostatically Sensitive Assemblies Can Be Destroyed By Discharges Below The Human Perception Threshold. These Discharges Occur, For Example, By Touching An Assembly Or Its Connections. The Damage May Also Only Occur After A Longer Period Of Operation. Basic Precautions To Protect Against Electrostatic Discharges:

Configuration Mode Description / Client Screen View Control Elements Add Allows The Creation Of A Product, Inspections, Exposures, Areas, References Or Contours. The Element Created Is Given A Name By The System, Which You Can Change.

Screen Layout - Center Load Product Image A Product Image Must Be Defined For Each Component In Order To Display An Image Of The Component To Be Inspected In Automatic Mode. If This Product Image Is To Be Used As An Error Map, The Bead Path On The Product Image Must Additionally Be Defined For Each Inspection.

Help Help Or F1 Opens The Online Help As A Book.

Min. Priority Selects The Priority Level For Displaying The Logbook Entries. Each Level Is Displayed With "Green", While Normal Messages Are Filtered With "Yellow", Which Are To Be Assigned To This And The Red Level. With "Red", Only Messages Marked In Red That Show Errors Are Displayed.
Close Closes The Logbook. View | Display History

Result Type This Filtering Is Used To Sort By The Respective Inspection Results. Blue Shows All Results Without Sorting, Green, Yellow And Red Sort By Ok, Warn And Nok Results. A Dark Red-Brown Color Indicates A System Error. This Can Also Occur, For Example, If The Storage Capacity Of The Hard Disk Does Not Allow Any Further Storage.
Automatic Mode / Logbook, History And Statistics Close Closes The Dialog.
Automatic Mode / Logbook, History And Statistics Control Elements For Statistics
Update Refresh Instigates A Manual Update Of The Display In Table Format, Whereas The Simple Statistics Are Automatically Updated With Each Inspection Run.
Automatic Mode / Logbook, History And Statistics
4. In **Storage Interval**, Specify The Data Recording Interval (In Milliseconds) For Inspecting The Component.

Chapter 6 Results Evaluation 6 Results Evaluation 6.1 The Viewer
Results Evaluation / The Viewer Viewer Controls
Control Elements In The Detailed View

System On / Off (2.0) Is Set To 1 When The Vision System Is Started. Automatic On / Off (2.1) Is Set To 1 When Automatic Mode Is Started. Product Selectable (2.2) Is Set To 1 When The System Is Expecting A Product To Be Selected. Inspection Selectable (2.3) Is Set To 1 When An Inspection Can Be Selected. Inspection Changeable (2.4) Is Set To 1 If An Inspection Can Be Changed. Result Available (2.5) Is Set To 1 If A Product Was Ended And Therefore A Result Is Available (Ok, Warn, Nok, Err).
Result Messages: The System Uses The Result Messages To Report The Result Of The Most Recent Run.
